Creating, Posting and
Promoting Social Media
Content
Created by Jeremy Haynes, Use By Some Of The World's Largest Personality Brands
Made For...
Personal Brands, Companies, And Anyone with High Quality Social Media Content That Wants To
Post and Maximize Reach On Multiple Social Media Channels, Including Facebook, Instagram,
Twitter, LinkedIn and Youtube.
Assets Needed
Content
1. You’ll want to have a long form piece of content, like a speech on stage, an interview, a
story telling video, a Q&A video answering fans questions, or anything that is longer than
10-15 min and provides real value to the audience you’ll be targeting
2. Daily clips that are 3-5 min can also work for this, and should be created with the intention
of being ‘belief management’ ‘future objection handling’ or ‘priming them with necessary
information to later make decisions with’ videos. See Dan Lok Facebook or Instagram page
for examples of this.
○ All content should be created with an intention, and should never be created just to
have something to post, as people won’t give a fuck about it if it doesn’t actually do
something for them, give them a feeling, or provide them a new way to look at the
world by managing their beliefs and perceptions. Always consider the selfish rule of
thumb, “Would I actually care to watch this if I wasn’t me?”
Video Editors & Graphic Designers
3. It’s best practices to have a video editor fluent in Adobe Premiere, Final Cut Pro, or
whatever preferred video editing software they use, bonus if they are also a graphic
designer and it will save you money.
4. The graphic designer is needed for helping the video editor, or so the video editor/graphic
designer can make creative images to be used as animations throughout your video
content. The graphic designer can also help style the videos and be helpful for keeping
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 7 --

2
branding consistent with the border styles, fonts, and general animation styles used
throughout your content.
Some Tools That Can Help
5. Shakr.com - helpful when you need some quick promos created for the content for cross
promoting to other channels or for previewing content on insta stories
6. https://appvemely.com/ - for creating styled content like Gary Vee
7. HypeText App - for creating image quotes
8. Typic App - for creating image quotes
9. Videoshop app / gravie app - both video editing apps for mobile editing
10. Legend App - for creating animations on mobile for promoting content
11. CropVS app - for cropping videos on mobile
12. Continual App - for when you shoot videos on snapchat longer than 15 seconds, save the
file, and then need it split up into 15 seconds segments to then be able to upload to
InstaStories
13. Agorapulse.com can help with managing multiple social accounts, promotional calendars,
and makes the process for a social media manager easier to be able to go to one dashboard
with all social channels and communication from those channels in one place
Notes
● Instagram allows for up to 60 seconds videos, however you can chop up multiple 60
second video clips and post with the swipe right feature to have multiple videos featured
in one post - you just need the full clip chopped up into multiple 60 seconds clips
● Facebook has a 3-4 hour limit on how long each video can be depending on your page type.
● LinkedIn has 10 minute video length max currently.
○ This is also a very different platform than other channels in regards to how the
users will interact with your brand, you’ll have less trolls and likely less engagement
here, however the engagement will be quality.
● Youtube has a 2 hour or 2 ½ hour video limit depending on your account and how many
subscribers you have, along with previous amount of videos posted to channel as well.
Action Items
1. If you’re starting off with a long form piece of content (a video longer than 10-15 min) like a
speech on stage, an interview, a story telling video, a Q&A video answering fans questions,
etc - then you’re going to follow the next steps.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 7 --

3
○ If you have a 3-5 min piece of content, some of these first steps will be able to get
applied to your video content, make the call or skip to later steps to go over
distribution, and cross channel promotion
○ How to take main video and chop it up for instagram, Facebook, Twitter and
LinkedIn:
○ STEP 1 - Download Video from Youtube Using "http://offliberty.com/" or any
other youtube to mp4 downloader that you prefer.
○ STEP 2 - Create a 1000x1000 Composition in premiere
○ STEP 3 - Import the downloaded youtube video into premiere and scale it down to
53%(depending if it’s 1920x1080 or 1280x720). Then Cut out the 30-60 sec clip I
give you.
○ STEP 4 - Apply a Color Matte Behind the video (blue, Black, or White) adjust
opacity @ 80% - 90% so the background is visible
○ STEP 5 - Google “lifestyle” images and select an image that is larger than
1000x1000. Add it to the timeline then scale it up to cover the screen. Then set a
position animation at the very beginning of the video, then another position
keyframe at the end of the video and move the image to the LEFT. Please apply a
5.0 Gaussion Blur to the background as well.
○ STEP 6 - Apply the Top Caption Title that I give you (Make sure to fill up a good
amount + all caps" (Look at https://www.instagram.com/ericcasaburi/ for
reference on size)
○ STEP 7 - Animate the 6-8 sentences I give you. *Some will be word for word
animation, some will be simple text blocks* CHECK SPELLING IN GOOGLE.
○ STEP 8 - Apply the Orange animating border. Then Change the Speed Duration to
fit the entire Video. Use “Change Color To” in Premeire to change the border color
to Blue or Keep it orange to contrast (depending on background color) MAKE
SURE the Border Scale size is 81.0 in Height and 78.5 in width.
○ STEP 9 - Render final edit, DOUBLE CHECK to make sure video is good AFTER the
render then send the video to whoever is posting.
2. Give the long form piece of content to your video editor, and have them chop up the
content into as many 1-3 minute QUALITY video clips as possible. Use styling steps 1-9 in
previous step description. They should be looking for key points made, stories, micro
moments, or clips that can be used for previews to the full thing leveraging curiosity bias.
○ The content should be interesting and something that viewers will actually want to
watch. If you end up with only 1-2 quality clips that can be pulled out from a 10
min-60 min video, that’s better than having 10 clips pulled out that suck.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 7 --

4
■ You’re going for quality, and are going to be spending money to distribute
these to an audience that will later be offered chances to buy from you. DO
NOT turn them off with shitty content.
3. Once all the clips are styled up, and exported, go to rev.com and upload the videos to get
caption files for each video that will be used.
○ You can then go back to video editor and have them ‘burn in’ the captions into the
video file itself, or with your new caption SRT file for each video--you can just hang
on to them to upload to Facebook and Youtube natively for them to handle adding
into your video.
■ Best practices here are to add it into the video after you get the SRT caption
files from rev.com, and to ensure the text isn’t bigger than 20% of the image
to text ratio, so you don’t get throttled on reach on the social media
platforms.
4. Once you have your content, prepare a content marketing plan for the week or for the
month, of what you’re going to be posting, and when.
○ Literally have a social media marketing calendar that your team members can
share, including the video editor, the character shooting the content, the social
media manager that is posting, and the digital marketer who will need to know
what to promote and when.
5. You’ll want to take your pieces of content that you’re ready to post for the week, and give
them to copywriter to turn into ‘long form content’
○ This is blog style content that can be posted on LinkedIn, and on your website blog,
created from the topics that are spoken about and created from your video you’ll
be posting on other social media channels
○ This can also be a great place to make call to actions in the footer of each article
created to either go watch videos on other platforms like Youtube, Facebook or
Instagram - in addition you can make call to actions for lead magnets, webinars and
sales offers here too.
■ You’ll need to have posted on the other channels in order to get the links to
cross promote too if you’re going to do this step.
○ Preferably have your long form content ready to post the same day on LinkedIn
and your blog, that you’ll be dropping on other social channels with the video that
pairs with the topic covered.
■ So if I have blog for Mon, Wed and Fri - I’m going to post the video that
matches that topic on Mon, Wed and Fri as well, so I can cross promote on
all channels and have consistency across all social media channels.
6. Now that you have your ‘long form copy’ that will be the articles for the week created, you
have your videos created and made into the different lengths for each platform like
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 7 --

5
Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, Youtube and Twitter, you’re ready to either schedule out
content, or go post multiple times per week as scheduled manually.
○ Each platform gives you the ability to schedule content through tools like
Agorapule.com, or natively on the platform
7. Once you post a piece of content on Facebook and Instagram, post the clips you’ll want to
have on Youtube at the same time.
○ Use Tubebuddy.com no Youtube to optimize the video tags, description, title, and
thumbnail with best practices to maximize the organic reach.
○ When posted on Facebook and Instagram, you’ll be running ‘engagement
campaigns’ and ‘video view campaigns optimized for ‘thruplay’’ after you’ve posted
the content
8. Be sure to also post your article on LinkedIn, and make separate posts for your videos on
LinkedIn as well.
○ You can take the new article link after published, and promote the article link with
your video content posts on LinkedIn to interconnect and promote your content
on that channel.
■ So publish article 1st on LinkedIn, then video posts so you have the article
link to promote with the videos at the bottom of your copy with each video.
9. Once you’ve posted your content on Facebook and Instagram, you’re going to want to
head over to the ads manager and follow the steps in either of these videos:
○ https://www.facebook.com/TheJeremyHaynes/videos/625867704455690/
○ https://www.facebook.com/TheJeremyHaynes/videos/662626787446448/
○ To summarize, you’re going to go into the ads manager after you’ve posted your
content on FB and IG, and press ‘create’ to initiate the process of creating a new
campaign.
■ You’re going to create 2 different campaigns, and all your future content
call be easily duplicated without having to create new campaigns by simply
clicking into the campaign objective after you do this step once, pressing
duplicate an ad set, changing the name of the ad set and ad to reflect
whatever piece of content you’re going to promote in this new duplicated
ad set, and then go to the ad level to swap out the post that you’re going to
be promoting.
■ After you have your engagement campaign and your video view campaign
created (making sure on the ad set level of your video view campaign that it
is optimized for ‘thruplay’ and to only charge you when a ‘thruplay’ occurs) -
select your cold audiences, or the warm audiences that you’re wanting to
promote content too.
● See the 2 videos above as examples and how to’s on this process
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 7 --

6
■ And publish the new campaigns after selecting the post you’re wanting to
promote and the audience that you’re wanting to target
○ Some best practices on budget per video, is between $5/day - $50/day depending
on budget and desired reach.
■ Some people want to go after large cold audiences who don’t know them
yet, and are willing to spend more money, faster to build those audiences in
less time
■ Some people have millions of people who have already been exposed to the
brand, and to keep yourself omnipresent and in front of this audience - you
may find you need to spend more money per post to keep it in front of your
entire audience
10. Go to twitter and promote your new content on other channels with links to your blog
posts, your linkedin article posts, your FB post, and your IG posts
○ Best practices here are to consider which out of all the channels you want to
promote to for that specific piece of content, and balance between all of the
channels without using every channel for every post.
■ So maybe Monday you tweet out the LinkedIn article, and the FB post for
your new piece of content, but Wed you promote on Twitter the IG post
and the Youtube video as an example.
○ You can also leverage tools like https://www.socialjukebox.com/ which acts like an
hourglass that you load up with content, it will cycle through tweeting that content,
and once it’s gone through everything in it that you’ve loaded up, it will ‘flip’ like an
hourglass promoting from the last thing to the first thing that’s been loaded into it
prior.
■ This can be highly effective as it provides a way to have tweets always going
out that are scheduled and enables all historical content to be used again in
the future
11. Congrats, you’ve now posted on multiple channels with a single piece of content in
different ways, and you’ve maximized the distribution of that content on the different
channels organically. Now let’s move onto cross channel promotion.
12. Let’s say that I want to use Facebook or Instagram to cross promote my new youtube
video, or my new LinkedIn article.
○ I can simply go to the Facebook page or instagram page, make a post and link to the
article or Youtube Video, and then run an engagement campaign on that new post
which would take traffic from Facebook or Instagram, and drive it over to Youtube
or LinkedIn.
■ This is a highly effective strategy for getting people from one social channel
to others that you have and is encouraged to hedge to the risk of getting
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 7 --

7
too large on one channel, and to also increase the viewership and fan bases
on all channels.
13. Let’s say that I have a new Youtube video or LinkedIn article, or Facebook video that I want
to promote using messenger stories, Instagram Stories or Facebook stories.
○ I could could create a preview of that new piece of content using a tool like the
Legend App, or Shakr.com to get a nice animated preview of whatever I’m going to
promote leveraging curiosity as the main bias that will drive users to swipe up
○ I’d then go to Instagram Stories, Messenger (by Facebook) and Facebook Stories (in
Facebook app for personal account and in Creators app for FB Page), and would
post the preview I’ve created and a link to whatever social channel I want to cross
promote to with a swipe up call to action
14. Let’s say I have a youtube video, and I want to use that to drive people to my LinkedIn or to
my Instagram or Facebook
○ Either in the video, I could add a bumper at the end that promotes going to these
other channels to subscribe to me there too
○ Or I could also just add links to all my social channels, or the most recent pieces of
content in each video in the description of every video that I am posting
Be sure to always consider the viewer, and not your selfish
intentions of getting people to go to each platform when cross
promoting.
You will need to know how to leverage real curiosity and other
human drivers to get people to actually want to go to the other
platforms to watch you and consume your content there as well.
Do this wrong and you’ll turn off the audience and lower your
engagement immensely.
Quality > Quantity.
Every post is strategic. Do not ever just post, to post.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
